                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Functions

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                class ConfigHelper

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • load_config(cls, file_path: Union[str, os.PathLike]="config/config.ini") – Load the configuration from ``file_path``. The file is read only when it's not cached or when the file has changed on disk since the last load. This allows updating the configuration without restarting the application. Parameters: file_path (type Union[str, os.PathLike], default "config/config.ini").
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • get(cls, section, key, fallback=None) – Return the operation. Parameters: section; key; fallback (default None).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • getboolean(cls, section, key, fallback=False) – Handle getboolean. Parameters: section; key; fallback (default False).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • set(cls, section, key, value, file_path: Union[str, os.PathLike, None]=None) – Set the operation. Parameters: section; key; value; file_path (type Union[str, os.PathLike, None], default None).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • get_config_path(cls) – Return config path. Returns: Path.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • get_campaign_dir(cls) – Return the directory containing the configured database file.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • get_campaign_settings_path(cls) – Path to campaign-local settings file stored next to the DB.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • load_campaign_config(cls) – Load campaign-local settings.ini, cached by mtime similar to load_config.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • _sanitize_ini(path: str) – Remove invalid empty-key assignments from an INI file. Parameters: path (type str). Returns: Union[str, None].

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  modules\helpers\customtkinter_compat.py

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Compatibility patches for CustomTkinter widgets used by the app.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Functions

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • apply_ctk_button_after_cleanup_patch(ctk_module: Any) – Ensure ``CTkButton`` cancels pending ``after`` callbacks on destroy. Some CustomTkinter builds can emit Tcl errors like ``invalid command name "..._click_animation"`` when a button is destroyed while a click animation callback is still queued. This patch tracks callback IDs created via ``after`` on each button instance and cancels them during ``destroy``. Parameters: ctk_module (type Any). Returns: None.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • _build_pattern_difficulties(match: re.Match[str], pattern: _ActionPattern, attack_bonus: str | None, attack_roll: str | None) – Build pattern difficulties. Parameters: match (type re.Match[str]); pattern (type _ActionPattern); attack_bonus (type str | None); attack_roll (type str | None). Returns: Tuple[ParsedDifficulty, ...].

Parameters: difficulties (type Iterable[ParsedDifficulty]). Returns: Tuple[ParsedDifficulty, ...].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • _combine_difficulties(*groups: Iterable[ParsedDifficulty]) – Internal helper for combine difficulties. Parameters: groups (variadic positional, type Iterable[ParsedDifficulty]). Returns: Tuple[ParsedDifficulty, ...].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • _spans_overlap(left: Tuple[int, int], right: Tuple[int, int]) – Internal helper for spans overlap. Parameters: left (type Tuple[int, int]); right (type Tuple[int, int]). Returns: bool.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • parse_inline_actions(raw_text: Any) – Extract inline combat actions from a block of text. Returns a tuple ``(display_text, actions, errors)`` where ``display_text`` is the original text with markup removed, ``actions`` is a list of dictionaries describing parsed actions, and ``errors`` captures any validation issues encountered. Parameters: raw_text (type Any). Returns: tuple[str, list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • _parse_segment(segment: str, span: Tuple[int, int], *, base_offset: int) – Parse segment. Parameters: segment (type str); span (type Tuple[int, int]); base_offset (keyword-only, type int). Returns: tuple[ParsedAction | None, ParsedError | None, str | None].

Parameters: label (keyword-only, type str); attack_bonus (keyword-only, type str | None); damage_formula (keyword-only, type str | None); notes (keyword-only, type str | None); base_offset (keyword-only, type int). Returns: tuple[str, Tuple[int, int] | None, Tuple[int, int] | None].

Parameters: left (type str). Returns: tuple[str, str | None, str | None].

Parameters: right (type str); span (type Tuple[int, int]); segment (type str). Returns: tuple[str | None, str | None, ParsedError | None].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • _split_dm_damage(text: str) – Internal helper for split dm damage. Parameters: text (type str). Returns: tuple[str, str | None].

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              class RandomTableLoader

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • __init__(self, base_path: Optional[str]=None) – Initialize the RandomTableLoader instance. Parameters: base_path (type Optional[str], default None).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • default_data_path() – Return the preferred random tables path. The loader looks for a directory first so tables can be split across multiple files. If no directory is found, it falls back to a single JSON file either in the app or the current campaign.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   class AnalyzerPattern

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Normalized representation of an analyzer pattern entry.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    class SystemConfig

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Fully parsed system configuration for the active campaign.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      class SystemConfigManager

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Loader and cache for campaign system configuration.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • get_current_system_config(cls) – Return the cached system configuration, refreshing if required. Returns: Optional[SystemConfig].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • refresh_current_system(cls) – Force a reload of the active system configuration. Returns: Optional[SystemConfig].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • set_current_system(cls, slug: str) – Persist a new active system slug and refresh the cache. Parameters: slug (type str). Returns: Optional[SystemConfig].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • list_available_systems(cls) – Return all configured systems parsed into :class:`SystemConfig` objects. Returns: Tuple[SystemConfig, ...].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • register_change_listener(cls, callback: ChangeCallback) – Register a callback invoked whenever the active system changes. Parameters: callback (type ChangeCallback). Returns: Callable[[], None].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • unregister_change_listener(cls, callback: ChangeCallback) – Remove a previously registered change listener. Parameters: callback (type ChangeCallback). Returns: None.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _refresh_cache(cls, *, force: bool) – Reload the cached configuration if required. Returns a tuple of (config, changed) indicating whether the cached configuration was updated. Parameters: force (keyword-only, type bool). Returns: Tuple[Optional[SystemConfig], bool].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _resolve_system_config(cls, slug: Optional[str]) – Load the system configuration for ``slug`` (falling back to defaults). Parameters: slug (type Optional[str]). Returns: Optional[SystemConfig].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _load_config_for_slug(cls, slug: str) – Load config for slug. Parameters: slug (type str). Returns: Optional[SystemConfig].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _load_first_system(cls) – Load first system. Returns: Optional[SystemConfig].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _fetch_all_system_rows(cls) – Internal helper for fetch all system rows. Returns: Sequence[Tuple[Any, ...]].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _row_to_config(cls, row: Optional[Sequence[Any]]) – Internal helper for row to config. Parameters: row (type Optional[Sequence[Any]]). Returns: Optional[SystemConfig].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _parse_supported_faces(raw_json: Optional[str]) – Parse supported faces. Parameters: raw_json (type Optional[str]). Returns: Tuple[FaceValue, ...].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _parse_analyzer_config(cls, raw_json: Optional[str]) – Parse analyzer config. Parameters: raw_json (type Optional[str]). Returns: Tuple[Tuple[AnalyzerPattern, ...], Mapping[str, Any]].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _mapping_proxy(data: Optional[Mapping[str, Any]]) – Internal helper for mapping proxy. Parameters: data (type Optional[Mapping[str, Any]]). Returns: Mapping[str, Any].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _system_exists(cls, slug: str) – Internal helper for system exists. Parameters: slug (type str). Returns: bool.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _compute_db_signature(cls) – Internal helper for compute DB signature. Returns: Tuple[Optional[str], Optional[float], Optional[int], Optional[int]].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _notify_listeners(cls, config: SystemConfig) – Notify listeners. Parameters: config (type SystemConfig). Returns: None.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      modules\helpers\template_loader.py

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Loading helpers for template.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Functions

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _default_template_path(entity_name: str) – Internal helper for default template path. Parameters: entity_name (type str). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _campaign_template_path(entity_name: str) – Internal helper for campaign template path. Parameters: entity_name (type str). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _template_path(entity_name: str) – Prefer campaign-local template if it exists, else fall back to defaults. Parameters: entity_name (type str). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _load_base_template(entity_name: str) – Load the current template JSON (campaign-local if present). Built-in templates are synchronized on read so runtime schema changes in the repo are reflected even if an older campaign-local copy already exists. Parameters: entity_name (type str). Returns: dict.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _custom_manifest_path() – Internal helper for custom manifest path. Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _load_custom_manifest() – Load custom manifest. Returns: dict.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _save_custom_manifest(manifest: dict) – Save custom manifest. Parameters: manifest (type dict).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _resolve_icon_path(icon_value: str | None) – Resolve icon path. Parameters: icon_value (type str | None). Returns: str | None.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _prepare_icon_for_entity(entity_slug: str, icon_source: str | None) – Internal helper for prepare icon for entity. Parameters: entity_slug (type str); icon_source (type str | None). Returns: str | None.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • load_entity_definitions() – Return mapping of entity slug to metadata (label, icon, is_custom). Returns: dict.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • load_template(entity_name: str) – Load template and return merged fields (fields + custom_fields if present). Built-in fields remain separate inside the file under "fields". User-added fields are persisted under "custom_fields" and merged at runtime for UI. Parameters: entity_name (type str). Returns: dict.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • create_custom_entity(entity_slug: str, display_name: str, icon_source: str | None=None) – Create a campaign-local template for ``entity_slug`` and register metadata. Parameters: entity_slug (type str); display_name (type str); icon_source (type str | None, default None). Returns: dict.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • update_custom_entity(entity_slug: str, display_name: str | None=None, *, icon_source: str | None=None, clear_icon: bool=False) – Update label and/or icon metadata for an existing custom entity. Parameters: entity_slug (type str); display_name (type str | None, default None); icon_source (keyword-only, type str | None, default None); clear_icon (keyword-only, type bool, default False). Returns: dict.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • delete_custom_entity(entity_slug: str) – Remove an existing custom entity definition and its template. Parameters: entity_slug (type str).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _render_template_content(fields: list, custom_fields: list) – Return the canonical JSON body for a template file. Parameters: fields (type list); custom_fields (type list). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _write_template_file(path: str, fields: list, custom_fields: list) – Write template content to ``path`` using canonical formatting. Parameters: path (type str); fields (type list); custom_fields (type list).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • save_custom_fields(entity_name: str, fields: list) – Write custom fields into the entity template file under "custom_fields" key. File format goal: keep the overall JSON pretty-printed with indent=2, but ensure each custom field object is rendered on a single line for clarity. Parameters: entity_name (type str); fields (type list).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • sync_campaign_template(entity_name: str) – Ensure the campaign template for ``entity_name`` matches the default fields. Returns ``True`` when the campaign template file was created or updated. Existing ``custom_fields`` are preserved when rewriting the file. Parameters: entity_name (type str). Returns: bool.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • list_known_entities() – Handle list known entities. Returns: list.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • list_manageable_entities() – Return known entities that should be shown in user-manageable UI lists. Returns: list.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • list_known_entity_labels() – Return display labels for every discovered entity template. Returns: list.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • build_entity_wrappers() – Return ``slug -> GenericModelWrapper`` for all known entities. Returns: dict.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      modules\helpers\text_helpers.py

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Utilities for text helpers.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Functions

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _mojibake_score(text: str) – Count obvious mojibake markers in a string. Parameters: text (type str). Returns: int.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _repair_mojibake_text(text: str) – Repair common UTF-8-vs-cp1252 mojibake without touching clean text. Parameters: text (type str). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • deserialize_possible_json(value) – Attempt to deserialize a JSON or Python literal string. Many data fields in the campaign database store longform content as serialized dictionaries (e.g. ``{"text": ..., "formatting": ...}``). Depending on the import path this data may arrive either as proper JSON or as the string representation of a Python dict that uses single quotes. To keep the UI code simple we normalize these payloads here. If ``value`` is a string that looks like JSON we first try ``json.loads``. When that fails we fall back to ``ast.literal_eval`` which safely handles Python literals (lists, dicts, numbers, strings). If deserialization is not possible we simply return the original value so the caller can treat it as a plain string. Parameters: value.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • rtf_to_html(rtf) – Convert RTF-JSON {"text":…, "formatting":…} into HTML, handling: - numeric offsets (e.g. "0", "26") - Tk indices (e.g. "1.26" for line 1, column 26) Parameters: rtf.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • normalize_rtf_json(rtf, text_widget=None) – Convert any { text:str, formatting:{ tag:[[s,e],…], … } } where s/e might be "line.char" (e.g. "1.26") into integer offsets. Parameters: rtf; text_widget (default None).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • ai_text_to_rtf_json(raw_text) – Convert an AI answer (plain text with light Markdown/HTML) to our RTF-JSON: {"text": str, "formatting": { tag: [(start,end), ...], ... }} Supported inline markers: - Bold: **text**, __text__, <b>text</b>, <strong>text</strong> - Italic: *text*, _text_, <i>text</i>, <em>text</em> - Underline: ++text++, <u>text</u> Block helpers: - Headings: #, ##, ### → size_22/18/16 on the full line text - Bullets: lines starting with "- " or "* " are turned into "• " prefix - Numbered: lines starting with "1. ", "2) ", etc. are kept as-is Returns formatting with numeric offsets suitable for RichTextEditor.load_text_data. Parameters: raw_text.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • _coerce_text(val) – Coerce text. Parameters: val.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • coerce_text(val) – Return a plain-text representation for longtext-style values. Many parts of the UI accept data that may either be a raw string or the richer ``{"text": ..., "formatting": ...}`` structure produced by importers. ``coerce_text`` normalises the value so widgets can safely display it without worrying about the underlying shape. Parameters: val.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • format_longtext(data, max_length=30000) – Format long text for list/table display. - Normalizes rich payloads via ``_coerce_text``. - Collapses line breaks to spaces for compact single-line rendering. - Truncates to ``max_length`` and appends ``...``. Parameters: data; max_length (default 30000).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • format_multiline_text(data, max_length=30000) – Format text for multi-line display (e.g. web ``pre-wrap`` blocks). - Normalizes rich payloads via ``_coerce_text``. - Preserves paragraph breaks by keeping ``\n`` line separators. - Truncates to ``max_length`` and appends an ellipsis. Parameters: data; max_length (default 30000).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      modules\helpers\theme_manager.py

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Management helpers for theme.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Functions

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • get_theme() – Return the current UI theme key, preferring campaign-local settings. Order of precedence: 1) Campaign settings.ini -> [UI] theme 2) Global config/config.ini -> [UI] theme 3) Built-in default Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • apply_theme(theme: str | None=None) – Apply the CustomTkinter color palette for the given theme key. Uses built-in CustomTkinter themes for simplicity. Can be swapped to custom JSON files later if desired. Parameters: theme (type str | None, default None). Returns: None.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • set_theme(theme: str) – Persist and apply a new theme for the current campaign, then notify listeners. Parameters: theme (type str). Returns: None.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • get_tokens(theme: str | None=None) – Return app color tokens that we use where CTk's palette isn't enough. These are intentionally minimal; expand as needed. Parameters: theme (type str | None, default None). Returns: dict.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • register_theme_change_listener(callback: Callable[[str], None]) – Register a callback invoked with the new theme key after changes. Returns an unsubscribe function. Parameters: callback (type Callable[[str], None]). Returns: Callable[[], None].

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Utilities for window helper.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Functions

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        • position_window_at_top(window, width=None, height=None) – Position a window at the top of the screen, centered horizontally. Args: window: the CustomTkinter or Tkinter window to position. width: largeur fixe (facultatif). Si None, utilise la taille actuelle. height: hauteur fixe (facultatif). Si None, utilise la taille actuelle. Parameters: window; width (default None); height (default None).

Parameters: query (type str | None). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • tokenize_query(query: str | None) – Tokenize query into deterministic terms for lookup/matching. Parameters: query (type str | None). Returns: list[str].

Parameters: name (type str | None). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • normalize_tag(tag: str | None) – Normalize one tag value. Parameters: tag (type str | None). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • normalize_extension(extension: str | None) – Normalize extension and remove leading dot. Parameters: extension (type str | None). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • build_search_tokens(*, name_normalized: str, tags: Iterable[str]) – Build deterministic search tokens from normalized name and tags. Parameters: name_normalized (keyword-only, type str); tags (keyword-only, type Iterable[str]). Returns: list[str].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • build_searchable_blob(*, name: str | None, path: str | None, relative_path: str | None, source_root: str | None, extension: str | None, tags: Iterable[str], name_normalized: str | None, search_tokens: Iterable[str], source_folder_name: str | None=None) – Build a lowercased precomputed blob used by fast in-memory matching. Parameters: name (keyword-only, type str | None); path (keyword-only, type str | None); relative_path (keyword-only, type str | None); source_root (keyword-only, type str | None); extension (keyword-only, type str | None); tags (keyword-only, type Iterable[str]); name_normalized (keyword-only, type str | None); search_tokens (keyword-only, type Iterable[str]); source_folder_name (keyword-only, type str | None, default None). Returns: str.

Parameters: repository (type ImageAssetsRepository | None, default None). Returns: None.

Parameters: payload (type dict[str, Any]). Returns: dict[str, Any].

Parameters: active_paths (type Iterable[str]). Returns: int.

Parameters: page (keyword-only, type int, default 1); page_size (keyword-only, type int, default 50); search (keyword-only, type str | None, default None). Returns: tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], int].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    • search_images(self, query: str | None=None, filters: ImageSearchFilters | dict[str, Any] | None=None, limit: int=50, offset: int=0, sort: SortOption="relevance") – Search image assets with rich filtering and UI-ready DTO results. Parameters: query (type str | None, default None); filters (type ImageSearchFilters | dict[str, Any] | None, default None); limit (type int, default 50); offset (type int, default 0); sort (type SortOption, default "relevance"). Returns: tuple[list[ImageAssetSearchResultDTO], int].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    • import_directories(self, paths: list[str], recursive: bool, reindex_changed_only: bool) – Import many directories through the dedicated import workflow. Parameters: paths (type list[str]); recursive (type bool); reindex_changed_only (type bool). Returns: ImageAssetsImportSummary.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          class ImageAssetImportService

Returns: None.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          • import_directories(self, paths: list[str], recursive: bool, reindex_changed_only: bool) – Import image assets from one or more roots. Args: paths: Root directories selected by user. recursive: If True, walk subdirectories; otherwise scan direct children only. reindex_changed_only: If True, keep unchanged records as-is. Parameters: paths (type list[str]); recursive (type bool); reindex_changed_only (type bool). Returns: ImageAssetsImportSummary.

Returns: list[str].

Returns: str.

Returns: tuple[int, int].

Returns: int | None.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          modules\image_assets\services\search_service.py

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Search service for image assets with in-memory and optional SQLite-FTS paths.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Structured filters accepted by `search_images`.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            • from_mapping(cls, value: dict[str, Any] | None) – Create filters from a plain mapping. Parameters: value (type dict[str, Any] | None). Returns: "ImageSearchFilters".

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            class ImageAssetSearchService

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Search image assets for UI consumers with optional FTS acceleration.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            • __init__(self, repository: ImageAssetsRepository | None=None) – Parameters: repository (type ImageAssetsRepository | None, default None). Returns: None.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            • search_images(self, query: str | None=None, filters: ImageSearchFilters | dict[str, Any] | None=None, limit: int=50, offset: int=0, sort: SortOption="relevance") – Search images for GM Screen and global image picker dialog. Parameters: query (type str | None, default None); filters (type ImageSearchFilters | dict[str, Any] | None, default None); limit (type int, default 50); offset (type int, default 0); sort (type SortOption, default "relevance"). Returns: tuple[list[ImageAssetSearchResultDTO], int].
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            • _search_in_memory(self, rows: list[dict[str, Any]], normalized_query: str) – Parameters: rows (type list[dict[str, Any]]); normalized_query (type str). Returns: list[dict[str, Any]].

Returns: list[dict[str, Any]] | None.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            • _matches_filters(self, row: dict[str, Any], filters: ImageSearchFilters) – Parameters: row (type dict[str, Any]); filters (type ImageSearchFilters). Returns: bool.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            • _sort_rows(self, rows: list[dict[str, Any]], *, sort: SortOption, query: str) – Parameters: rows (type list[dict[str, Any]]); sort (keyword-only, type SortOption); query (keyword-only, type str). Returns: list[dict[str, Any]].

Returns: dict[str, Any].

Returns: str.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • _resolve_campaign_path(path: str) – Return an absolute path for campaign-relative assets. Parameters: path (type str). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • _campaign_relative_path(path: str) – Internal helper for campaign relative path. Parameters: path (type str). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • _find_existing_map_image(original_path: str) – Attempt to locate a missing map background within the campaign. Parameters: original_path (type str). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • _default_token_image_path() – Locate the default pin image shipped with the application. Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • _find_existing_token_image(original_path: str) – Attempt to locate a token image inside the current campaign. Some persisted databases contain absolute paths from other machines. When those paths are reloaded we try to discover a matching asset inside the active campaign directory by progressively stripping leading folders and finally falling back to a filename search beneath the campaign's assets directory. Returns an absolute path if a match is found, otherwise an empty string. Parameters: original_path (type str). Returns: str.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • select_map(self) – Show the full‐frame map selector, replacing any existing UI.

Parameters: entity_type; map_name.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • close_web_display(self, port=None) – Shut down the web display server if it is running. The server is stopped using Werkzeug's ``shutdown`` API rather than sending an HTTP request to a dedicated route. Parameters: port (default None).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • _default_pin_image_path() – Return the absolute path to the bundled pin placeholder image. Returns: str.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                class WorldMapPanel (ctk.CTkFrame)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Interactive world map panel with nested map support. Can be embedded in other views (e.g., GM screen tabs). For legacy callers, see WorldMapWindow below, which wraps this panel in a CTkToplevel.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • __init__(self, master=None) – Initialize the WorldMapPanel instance. Parameters: master (default None).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                class WorldMapWindow (ctk.CTkToplevel)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Thin wrapper window that hosts a WorldMapPanel for legacy callers.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • __init__(self, master=None, initial_map: str | None=None) – Initialize the WorldMapWindow instance. Parameters: master (default None); initial_map (type str | None, default None).
